leadership_involvement: bring this to the exec team

preferred_question_routing.description: When pc-015 fires, prefer political/committee/EB mapping questions over generic workflow discovery. Only fall to workflow questions if no political mapping question is available.

priority_questions.question: When leadership evaluates something like this, what usually matters most to them?
Is this the kind of decision where one strong objection can stall it?
Who tends to have the strongest opinion when this gets discussed internally?
Whose support would matter most before this goes upstairs?
Who would be the hardest person to get comfortable with a change like this?
What needs to be true before this gets taken to the CFO or partners?
Who influences this decision even if they are not in the room?
Who tends to be the skeptical voice when something new is proposed?

pattern_clusters.canonical_label: Buying Committee Risk — 'This would go to committee'
pattern_clusters.description: The buyer reveals that the purchasing decision involves multiple stakeholders, committees, or consensus-driven processes. This exposes hidden veto players, internal blockers, stakeholder disengagement, and 'deal vampires' — people who consume time and resources without advancing the deal. The cluster detects committee references, delayed leadership exposure, proof-of-concept prerequisites, internal validation requirements, and consensus language. The rep must map the full political structure before the deal enters committee.
buyer_language_examples: committee would need to sign off
a few people would need to weigh in
need to get everyone on board
lot of people involved in this
operations would need to weigh in
tax team would want to look
if they push back it's done
review this with the team first
want the team to see it
don't move fast on decisions like this
takes time to get everyone comfortable
before bringing it to the CFO
people on the team will be skeptical
used to be in our position

decision_rules.selection_logic: When committee risk language appears, the rep must map the full political structure BEFORE the deal enters committee. Surface advancing questions that identify: (1) who has veto power, (2) whose opinion carries the most weight, (3) who resists change, (4) who champions change, (5) what matters most to leadership. Escalate to a reframe that positions early leadership engagement as risk reduction, not presumptuousness.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Who tends to have the strongest opinion when decisions like this come up internally?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Identify the most influential stakeholder — may or may not be the EB.

committee_mapping_questions.question: When something like this reaches leadership, what usually matters most to them — is it ROI, risk reduction, or something else?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Map the EB's evaluation criteria before the champion presents internally.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Is this the type of decision where one strong objection can stall the project?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Identify veto risk and consensus requirements.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Who internally tends to push for change and who tends to push back?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Map the change coalition and resistance network.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Whose opinion usually carries the most weight in decisions like this?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Identify the true power center — may differ from the org chart.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Who would be the hardest person internally to get comfortable with a change like this?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Pre-identify the primary blocker before they surface.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Whose support would make the biggest difference in getting something like this approved?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Identify the kingmaker — the person whose endorsement moves the committee.

committee_mapping_questions.question: Who internally tends to influence these decisions even if they aren't the final decision maker?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Map shadow influence — the informal power structure.

committee_mapping_questions.question: If this moves forward, who would we want engaged early to avoid surprises later?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Identify stakeholders who become blockers when excluded.

committee_mapping_questions.question: How does your team typically get from 'this looks interesting' to 'let's do this' — what are the steps in between?
committee_mapping_questions.intent: Map the full decision timeline and gate structure.

committee_reframes.text: That makes sense. In situations like this we usually find it helpful to understand what matters most to leadership early — so we don't spend time solving the wrong problem.
committee_reframes.intent: Redirect from 'prove it first' to 'let's learn what they care about first.'

committee_reframes.text: Totally fair. Sometimes the biggest risk is designing something that works operationally but doesn't align with what leadership actually cares about. Can I ask what their top priorities are right now?
committee_reframes.intent: Frame early EB understanding as protecting the champion's internal credibility.

committee_reframes.text: That happens a lot. One thing we try to do early is understand what success looks like from the executive perspective — so when this does reach them, it's already framed in their language.
committee_reframes.intent: Position Maybern as helping the champion succeed internally.

committee_reframes.text: I've seen deals move twice as fast when the right stakeholders are looped in early — not to pitch them, just to understand what they'd need to see. Would that approach work here?
committee_reframes.intent: Reframe early engagement as acceleration, not intrusion.

committee_reframes.text: The firms that move fastest are usually the ones where we understand the committee's priorities early enough to build the case around them — rather than rebuilding it after the first meeting.
committee_reframes.intent: Create urgency around early alignment as time savings.

decision_engine.description: 13-step evaluation order with judgment and composition layers. Steps 0-8 are the pattern+reasoning+judgment engine. Step 9 is the response composition engine that blends 4 knowledge sources (stories, product, ICP, ecosystem) into structured composition guides. The full loop: speaker filter → post-move classification → posture assessment → cluster matching → decision rules → stage lock → content selection → vendor enrichment → move evaluation → principle filter → question filter → escalation/fallback → response composition.

evaluation_order.name: SPEAKER FILTER
evaluation_order.description: Classify the current transcript segment using speaker_detection rules. Only proceed if speaker = 'client'.

evaluation_order.name: POST-MOVE CLASSIFICATION
evaluation_order.description: If a card was surfaced on the previous turn, classify the buyer's response into one of 5 buckets: engaged, neutral, deflected, resisted, advanced. Update move_affinity scores and feed result into posture_assessment. This is the FEEDBACK LOOP — the system learns what works with this specific buyer.

evaluation_order.name: POSTURE ASSESSMENT
evaluation_order.description: Classify the buyer's current conversational posture from accumulated signal state + post-move result + posture history. Output: primary posture, optional modifier, confidence score. Detect posture transitions and surface coaching notes when transitions occur.

evaluation_order.name: MATCH CLUSTER
evaluation_order.description: Scan transcript_buffer against pattern_clusters. Use signal_bridge to translate conversation_signals to cluster confidence_signals. Resolve ties using effective_weight from cluster_reinforcement.

evaluation_order.name: STAGE PRIORITY LOCK
evaluation_order.description: Check preferred_meeting_stages against current stage. Block future-stage content and execute stage_conversion_protocol if needed. Exceptions: competitor responses and objection responses are never blocked.

evaluation_order.name: SELECT CONTENT
evaluation_order.description: Based on recommended_move, select content from matched cluster's linked_item_ids or current stage's content arrays.

evaluation_order.name: VENDOR ASSET ENRICHMENT
evaluation_order.description: If a vendor was detected, look up canonical_conversation_assets and enrich the card.

evaluation_order.name: MOVE EVALUATION (JUDGMENT)
evaluation_order.description: After scoring candidates, evaluate whether the top-scored move is actually the right move given buyer posture, move history, and situational context. Apply conditional rules: stop hammering defensive buyers, don't question after acknowledgment, back off politically cautious buyers, block next steps without EB, etc. Adjust scores or block/substitute moves. This is where JUDGMENT overrides PREDICTION.

evaluation_order.name: APPLY PRINCIPLE FILTER
evaluation_order.description: If multiple candidates remain, prefer the one whose principle_tags include the cluster's preferred_principle.

evaluation_order.name: APPLY QUESTION TYPE FILTER
evaluation_order.description: If the move is a question, filter by preferred_question_type.

evaluation_order.name: ESCALATION
evaluation_order.description: If primary move already shown (cooldown), use escalation_move.

evaluation_order.name: FALLBACK
evaluation_order.description: If no cluster matches, use the stage's predictive_queue. Posture assessment and move_evaluation still apply to fallback selections.
evaluation_order.changed_in_v2: Modified — judgment layers now apply to fallback selections too

evaluation_order.name: RESPONSE COMPOSITION
evaluation_order.description: After the decision engine selects the move and content, and judgment layers have validated or adjusted it, the response composition engine assembles a multi-source composition guide. It blends the base card with story elements, product positioning, ICP context, ecosystem awareness, and posture-modulated tone. Output is a structured composition_guide — not raw text. The card renderer uses this guide to build the final teleprompter card.
evaluation_order.pipeline: 8-step composition pipeline: base_card_selection → story_element_retrieval → product_positioning_overlay → icp_contextualization → ecosystem_enrichment → posture_modulation → move_evaluation_gate → assembly
evaluation_order.fallback: If all enrichment layers return empty, card renders from base_card + tone only (v3 behavior).

move_types.advancing_question: Surface the next unasked DQ matching the preferred principle and question_type='advancing'. Use the stage's discovery_questions array, filtered by dq_status='unasked'.
move_types.confirming_question: Surface a DQ with question_type='confirming'. These appear at stage boundaries. If all confirming DQs are asked, surface next_step_language instead.
move_types.reframe: Surface a reframing_line from the current stage. Match by linked_item_ids if available, otherwise use the next unshown reframe in order.
move_types.objection_response: Surface from likely_objections or objection_handling_paths. Match the trigger field against transcript. If no stage-level match, fall through to top-level objection_handling.
move_types.competitor_response: Surface from competitor_response_paths. Match by competitor name in trigger field. If no stage-level match, fall through to top-level competitor_responses.
move_types.roi_prompt: Surface from roi_construction_prompts (M4 only). Walk in sequence. If outside M4, surface a reframing_line about cost instead.
move_types.next_step_lock: Surface from next_step_language. This is the 'book the meeting' move. Only fire when the belief shift is confirmed or the stage is ready to advance.

decision_engine.priority_resolution: When multiple clusters match simultaneously (e.g., buyer mentions a competitor AND expresses pricing concern), the cluster with the higher priority_weight wins. Ties broken by: (1) stage relevance match, (2) recency of trigger match, (3) array position in pattern_clusters.
decision_engine.processing_model: All logic executes as JSON lookups against the brain. The transcript_buffer is scanned client-side via string matching against buyer_language_examples and trigger_phrases. No server calls, no LLM inference, no external dependencies.

judgment_loop.description: The three judgment layers form a closed feedback loop that runs every turn. Post-move classifier evaluates whether the last move worked → feeds into posture assessment which updates the buyer model → feeds into move evaluation which adjusts the next card selection → the card is surfaced → next turn, post-move classifier evaluates the result. This loop means the system gets smarter about THIS SPECIFIC BUYER as the conversation progresses.
loop_sequence: 1. Card surfaced to rep (end of previous turn)
4. Post-move classifier evaluates buyer's reaction to last card (Step 0a) → writes result to session state
5. Posture assessment reads accumulated signals + post-move result → classifies buyer posture (Step 0b) → writes posture to session state
6. Pattern matching and content selection run (Steps 1-4a) → produces candidate cards
7. Move evaluation reads posture + move history + affinity scores → adjusts candidate scoring (Step 4b) → may block or substitute moves
9. Loop repeats on next buyer turn
judgment_loop.convergence_behavior: Over the course of a conversation, the feedback loop converges on the moves that work with this buyer. If stories consistently produce 'engaged' responses and reframes consistently produce 'deflected' responses, the system will naturally favor stories — not because someone pre-configured it, but because the loop learned it in real-time.
judgment_loop.latency: All three judgment layers combined add <15ms to the evaluation cycle. The loop runs at the speed of phrase matching, not inference.
judgment_loop.composition_integration: The composition engine sits at the end of the judgment loop. It receives posture state, move evaluation results, and post-move classification history. These inputs modulate how story elements, product positioning, and ecosystem context are assembled into the composition guide. The loop is now: post_move_classifier → posture_assessment → move_evaluation → response_composition_engine → card render → buyer response → post_move_classifier (repeat).

knowledge_composition_layer.description: Documentation for the composition layer added in Decision Engine v3.0.
knowledge_composition_layer.what_it_does: Transforms a raw content selection (Step 4) into a rich, multi-source composition guide by querying 4 knowledge registries and applying posture-modulated tone.
what_it_does_not_do: Does not generate natural language — it assembles ingredients
Does not override move_evaluation decisions — it respects judgment gates
Does not replace existing card content — it enriches it
Does not require API calls — all knowledge sources are local JSON
knowledge_composition_layer.performance_impact: All knowledge lookups are dictionary/array operations on in-memory JSON. Estimated additional latency: <5ms per composition. Total decision engine path including judgment + composition: <25ms.

cross_references.story_intelligence: Provides element-indexed story retrieval with 7 element types and multi-dimensional tagging
cross_references.product_knowledge: Provides stage-gated positioning (4 levels) and 6 value narratives
cross_references.icp_knowledge: Provides 4 segments with composition guidance and 5 fund type overlays
cross_references.ecosystem_map: Provides entity classification across 7 ecosystem layers with stage-specific composition guidance
cross_references.posture_assessment: Provides buyer posture state for tone modulation (9 posture types)
cross_references.move_evaluation: Provides judgment gates that can suppress or modify compositions (12 rules)
cross_references.post_move_classifier: Provides feedback on how buyer responded to previous composition (5 buckets)

priority_scoring.description: Scores every unasked question in the current stage to determine display priority. Runs locally from JSON fields. No APIs required.

meeting_stage_match.description: Does the question belong to the current meeting and stage?

scoring_rules.condition: question.meeting_relevance includes current_meeting_id but stage doesn't match

meeting_stage_match.rationale: Highest weight. A question designed for m1-advancing should not surface during m4-diagnose. This is the gate — if it scores 0, the question is excluded entirely.

signal_cluster_match.description: Do the question's conversation_signals overlap with the currently active pattern cluster's confidence_signals (via signal_bridge)?

scoring_rules.condition: 2+ signals overlap with active cluster (via signal_bridge)
question's signals overlap with ANY detected buyer language (no cluster match)

signal_cluster_match.rationale: Second-highest weight. When the buyer says 'we're fine' and pc-001 fires, questions with status_quo_confidence or status_quo_defense signals should jump to the top.

principle_match.description: Do the question's principle_tags align with the active cluster's decision_rules.preferred_principle?

scoring_rules.condition: question.principle_tags includes any principle relevant to the current stage's belief_shift_target

principle_match.rationale: Ensures the question strategically matches what the cluster is trying to accomplish. When pc-001 fires with preferred_principle=reframe_status_quo, questions tagged reframe_status_quo or create_future_contrast score highest.

advancing_vs_confirming_fit.description: Does the question's type match what the conversation needs right now?

scoring_rules.condition: question.question_type is 'confirming' AND stage is a confirming stage (m1-confirming, m2-confirming, m4-advance)
question.question_type is 'advancing' AND less than 60% of stage DQs are asked
question.question_type is 'confirming' AND more than 80% of stage DQs are asked
type mismatch (e.g., confirming question when cluster wants advancing)

advancing_vs_confirming_fit.rationale: Prevents confirming questions from surfacing too early (before discovery is done) and advancing questions from surfacing too late (after the belief shift has landed).

meddpicc_relevance.description: Do the question's MEDDPICC tags address a gap in the current deal's MEDDPICC coverage?

scoring_rules.condition: question.methodology_tags includes a MEDDPICC element that has NOT been addressed in this session
question.methodology_tags includes a MEDDPICC element addressed but only partially (1 question asked)
question.methodology_tags includes only fully-addressed MEDDPICC elements

meddpicc_relevance.rationale: Ensures MEDDPICC coverage across the deal. If no Champion question has been asked, a question tagged MEDDPICC-champion gets a boost. Tracks coverage across the session, not just the current stage.

display_rules.description: After scoring all unasked questions in the current stage, the teleprompter displays exactly 3 cards.

card_slots.label: RECOMMENDED
card_slots.selection: Highest-scoring advancing question. This is the main card the rep sees.
card_slots.fallback: If no advancing questions remain unasked, show the highest-scoring confirming question instead.

card_slots.label: WHEN READY
card_slots.selection: Highest-scoring confirming question. Shown dimmed below the primary card. Becomes primary when rep signals readiness to advance.
card_slots.fallback: If no confirming questions remain (all asked), show next_step_language from the current stage.

card_slots.label: ALSO CONSIDER
card_slots.selection: Second-highest-scoring question of either type that is NOT the same principle_tag as the primary. Ensures variety.
card_slots.fallback: If only 1 unasked question remains, this slot is empty.

display_rules.refresh_trigger: Re-score all candidates whenever: (1) a question is marked asked/skipped, (2) a new trigger fires, (3) a new cluster activates, (4) the rep advances to a new stage.
display_rules.score_visibility: Scores are internal. The rep sees the cards ranked, not the numbers.

session_state_requirements.active_cluster: The pattern_cluster currently matched by transcript, or null
session_state_requirements.detected_signals: Array of conversation_signals detected in the transcript buffer
session_state_requirements.meddpicc_coverage: Map of MEDDPICC element → count of questions asked for that element. e.g., { pain: 3, metrics: 1, champion: 0, economic_buyer: 0, decision_process: 0, decision_criteria: 0, competition: 0, paper_process: 0 }

speaker_detection.description: Simple speaker filtering rule that runs before trigger evaluation. Classifies each speech segment as sales_rep or client using first-message detection and turn alternation. Trigger evaluation runs only on client speech.

first_message_rule.description: Classify the first speech segment (message_index == 0) by content. The sales rep almost always opens the call.
first_message_rule.logic: If the first segment contains ONLY greeting phrases, assign client. Otherwise assign sales_rep.
first_message_rule.classification: If text.trim() matches or consists entirely of greeting phrases (after stripping punctuation), assign speaker = 'client'. Otherwise assign speaker = 'sales_rep'.

turn_alternation_rule.description: After the first message, assume speakers alternate when a clear turn or pause occurs.
turn_alternation_rule.logic: When a silence gap of 1500ms+ is detected between speech segments, check consecutive_turn_override conditions BEFORE flipping. If the new segment is a short acknowledgment (≤4 words, matches override phrases), keep the current speaker. If the new segment continues the same topic (starts with conjunction, shares vocabulary), keep the current speaker. If the pause is 800-1499ms, keep the current speaker (mid-thought pause). Only flip when: (a) the pause is ≥1500ms AND no override condition is met, OR (b) a handoff signal is detected regardless of pause length.
turn_alternation_rule.note: This is a simplification. It works well for two-person calls (rep + one client). For multi-person calls, the alternation may misassign — those segments should be treated as uncertain.

trigger_gate.description: Only evaluate trigger_phrases, buyer_language_examples, and conversation_signals when the current segment is classified as client.

rules.speaker: client
rules.action: EVALUATE — run full trigger detection, cluster matching, and priority scoring

rules.speaker: sales_rep
rules.action: IGNORE — skip all trigger detection for this segment. Do not scan for trigger_phrases, buyer_language_examples, or conversation_signals.

rules.speaker: uncertain
rules.action: HOLD — do not fire triggers. Buffer the segment. Do not surface any teleprompter cards based on this segment.

safety_fallback.description: When speaker cannot be confidently determined, treat the segment as neutral.
conditions: No silence gap preceded the segment (crosstalk)
Turn alternation produces an implausible assignment (e.g., 5 consecutive 'client' turns with no rep speech)
safety_fallback.action: Assign speaker = 'uncertain'. Do not fire triggers. Do not surface cards. Continue listening for the next segment.
safety_fallback.retroactive_reclassification: If the next segment resolves the speaker assignment (e.g., a clear rep question follows), retroactively reclassify the uncertain segment and process it if it was client speech.

consecutive_turn_override.description: Prevents the turn alternation rule from flipping the speaker when the next segment appears to continue the same speaker's thought. Short acknowledgments and topic continuations do not count as speaker changes.

override_conditions.description: Segment is a brief verbal filler that does not indicate a new speaker has taken the floor.
override_conditions.action: KEEP current speaker. Do not flip. Do not evaluate triggers (segment is too short to be meaningful).

override_conditions.description: Segment continues the same thought as the previous segment from the same speaker — same topic, no question directed at the other party, no handoff language.
continuation_signals: Segment starts with a conjunction or continuation word: 'and', 'so', 'but', 'also', 'plus', 'because', 'which', 'that said'
Segment starts with a self-correction: 'I mean', 'well actually', 'let me rephrase'
Segment continues a list or enumeration: 'the second thing is', 'another example', 'on top of that'
Segment shares 3+ non-trivial words with the previous segment from the same speaker (topic overlap)
override_conditions.action: KEEP current speaker. Evaluate triggers only if current speaker is client.

override_conditions.description: A pause occurred but it was shorter than the handoff threshold. People pause mid-thought. Only pauses at or above the threshold indicate a likely speaker change.
override_conditions.action: KEEP current speaker. This is a mid-thought pause, not a turn change.

handoff_signals.description: Even when an override condition is met, these signals force a speaker flip because they indicate a deliberate handoff.
signals: Previous segment ends with a direct question ('?')
Previous segment contains handoff language: 'what do you think', 'does that make sense', 'over to you', 'your turn', 'go ahead'
Previous segment is the rep asking a discovery question (matched against DQ text)
New segment begins with an answer to a question: 'well', 'honestly', 'that's a good question', 'so for us'
handoff_signals.action: FLIP speaker regardless of override conditions. A direct question demands a speaker change.

trigger_firing_rules.description: Rules governing when trigger_phrases are considered a match. Reduces false positives from generic single-word signals.

single_word_matching.allowed_for: Proper nouns only — competitor names, vendor names, admin names (Carta, Yardi, Allvue, SS&C, SEI, etc.)
single_word_matching.not_allowed_for: Generic words (excel, audit, price, manual, budget, etc.). These require contextual phrase matching.

contextual_phrase_matching.description: For generic terms, the trigger engine matches multi-word phrases instead of single words.
contextual_phrase_matching.example: 'excel waterfall' matches. 'excel' alone does not match. 'we use excel for reconciliation' matches both 'we use excel' and 'excel reconciliation'.

supporting_signal_only.description: Some words on trigger_conditions have supporting_signal_only = true. These words can contribute to a trigger score but cannot fire a trigger alone.
supporting_signal_only.logic: If a segment contains ONLY supporting_signal_only words and no full contextual phrase match, the trigger does NOT fire. The supporting word increases the confidence score if a contextual phrase match also fires on the same segment.
supporting_signal_only.example: 'hours' is supporting_signal_only on tr-047. 'We spend 40 hours per quarter' does NOT fire tr-047 from 'hours' alone. But 'We spend 40 hours per quarter on manual reconciliation' fires tr-055 (from 'manual reconciliation') and 'hours' boosts the confidence score.

minimum_match_threshold.description: For triggers with only generic phrases, require at least one multi-word phrase match OR two single-word supporting signals in the same segment.

supporting_signal_only_phrases: we have a version of that
the source of truth is actually
by the time we see it
we trust them to get it right
they've been doing it for years
we wouldn't know how to do it ourselves
we're pretty removed from that process
if you change one thing everything breaks
it works but I don't know why
it's only a matter of time
we're the ones who make it all work together
there's no single place to see everything
depends on which system you look at
every system tells a slightly different story
we spend more time moving data than using it
we've been down this road before
we looked at this a few years ago
the last time we tried to change something
promise me you're not just another vendor
those two weeks are basically lost
that's a good question — I'm not sure
a few people would need to weigh in
it's not really anyone's job specifically
it kind of falls between teams
we thought we could do it ourselves
it seemed simpler to just build it
our CTO wanted to keep it in-house
the team preferred to own the code
it started as a side project
it was supposed to be temporary

vendor_asset_integration.description: Rules for how the teleprompter references vendor intelligence and canonical conversation assets when a vendor or competitor signal appears during a live call. This layer sits alongside the existing trigger → cluster → decision_rules pipeline. It does not replace any existing content — it enriches the card the rep sees.

external_files.note: These files are loaded at session start and held in memory. No API calls required. The teleprompter references them by vendor_name lookup.

vendor_detection.description: When a client speech segment passes the speaker filter (Step 0), the system checks for vendor mentions BEFORE running the standard trigger → cluster pipeline. Vendor detection runs in parallel with trigger detection, not instead of it.
matching_rules: 1. EXACT NAME MATCH: Check the segment against vendor_name and aliases from the canonical_conversation_assets file. Case-insensitive. 'We use Investran' → matches 'Investran'.
2. TRIGGER PHRASE MATCH: If the segment fires an existing trigger_condition with recommended_response_type = 'competitor_response', extract the vendor name from the trigger's linked_content field.
3. CLUSTER MATCH: If pattern_cluster pc-003 (Competitor) or pc-004 (Internal Build) fires, check buyer_language_examples for vendor identification.
4. NO MATCH: If no vendor is identified, the standard pipeline continues without vendor enrichment.
vendor_detection.priority: Vendor detection does NOT override the decision_rules pipeline. It enriches it. The decision engine still determines the recommended_move (competitor_response, reframe, advancing_question, etc.). The vendor asset integration determines WHICH canonical asset populates the card.

asset_selection_rules.description: Once a vendor is identified, the system selects a canonical asset based on the decision engine's recommended_move. The canonical asset replaces or enriches the card content.

competitor_response.logic: Surface the first unused positioning_response for this vendor. If all have been shown in this session, fall back to rebuttals.

advancing_question.logic: Surface the first unused discovery_prompt for this vendor. These are vendor-specific questions that probe the pain the vendor creates. Supplement, do not replace, the stage's standard discovery questions.

reframe.logic: Surface the first unused rebuttal for this vendor. Rebuttals are designed to reframe vendor-as-objection into vendor-as-context.

objection_response.logic: When the vendor mention is framed as an objection ('we already have X'), surface a rebuttal first. If rebuttals are exhausted, surface a credibility story that demonstrates Maybern working alongside that vendor.

roi_prompt.logic: When vendor context supports an ROI discussion, surface a credibility story that grounds the ROI in real-world evidence.

next_step_lock.logic: When moving to next steps and the prospect uses this vendor, surface an integration explanation that shows how Maybern works alongside their existing stack.

session_tracking.description: Track which canonical assets have been shown per vendor per session to avoid repetition.
session_tracking.data_structure: Map of vendor_name → Map of asset_category → Set of shown indices
session_tracking.rotation_rule: Always show the lowest-indexed unshown asset. When all assets in a category are exhausted, stop showing that category — do not repeat.

card_composition.description: When a vendor asset is selected, the teleprompter card is composed from the canonical asset plus contextual metadata from the vendor registry.

card_structure.line_3_asset: The canonical asset text — the spoken-language snippet the rep delivers
card_structure.line_4_context: Optional one-line context from vendor_intelligence_registry.swot or client_feedback_mentions. Shown dimmed below the main asset. Gives the rep background without cluttering the primary card.

example_cards.scenario: Buyer says 'we run Investran' during M1/m1-advancing
example_cards.trigger: tr-007 fires (competitor mention) + vendor detected: Investran

card.label: VENDOR RESPONSE
card.asset: We don't compete with Investran. Investran is your GL — it tracks what happened. Maybern is your calculation engine — it computes what should happen. They serve different purposes.
card.context: GTCR and CD&R both use Investran — data accessibility is the consistent pain point.

example_cards.scenario: Buyer says 'our admin is Alter Domus' during M1/m1-advancing
example_cards.trigger: tr-006 fires (fund admin mention) + vendor detected: Alter Domus
example_cards.decision_rules: No competitor cluster fires (Alter Domus is ancillary_tool). Trigger recommends discovery_question.

card.label: VENDOR DISCOVERY
card.asset: When Alter Domus delivers your quarterly statements, does your team validate those calculations independently before they go out?
card.context: Universal pattern: GP controller spends 1-2 weeks validating admin waterfall output in Excel.

example_cards.scenario: Buyer says 'we already use Allvue' as objection during M2/m2-advancing
example_cards.trigger: tr-028 fires (competitor comparison) + vendor detected: Allvue
example_cards.decision_rules: pc-003 (Competitor) → recommended_move: competitor_response → asset mapping: positioning_responses

card.label: VENDOR RESPONSE
card.asset: Allvue is a solid GL and portal platform. Where firms run into limits is waterfall complexity and custom performance calculations. That's exactly the layer Maybern adds — you keep Allvue, you add Maybern for the math.
card.context: Multiple firms keep Excel shadow books on top of Allvue for waterfall calculations.

example_cards.scenario: Buyer says 'we use Yardi' during discussion of next steps in M2/m2-confirming
example_cards.trigger: Vendor detected: Yardi. Stage is confirming → recommended_move: next_step_lock
example_cards.decision_rules: No competitor cluster fires (Yardi is system_of_record). Move type: next_step_lock → asset mapping: integration_explanations

card.label: VENDOR INTEGRATION
card.asset: We have a production Yardi GL integration. You export from Yardi Investment Manager, upload to Maybern, and we automatically map entities, transaction codes, and accounts. Invalid rows get flagged with clear explanations before you commit.
card.context: 4 active Yardi integration projects. Full Maybern Docs guide exists.

example_cards.scenario: Buyer says 'we built our own system' during M1/m1-advancing
example_cards.trigger: tr-014 fires (internal build) + vendor detected: Internal Build
example_cards.decision_rules: pc-004 (Internal Solution) → recommended_move: reframe → asset mapping: rebuttals

card.label: VENDOR REFRAME
card.asset: That system was built for the fund structures that existed at the time. What happens when a new fund has a different waterfall, a different fee arrangement, or a continuation vehicle? Does the system adapt, or does your team re-code?
card.context: Golub's CATS system constrained product team. Thrive COO concluded 'owning infrastructure is not what we want to build.'

example_cards.scenario: Buyer says 'we use Chronograph for portfolio data' during M4/m4-diagnose
example_cards.trigger: Vendor detected: Chronograph. No competitor cluster fires (integration_partner category).
example_cards.decision_rules: No cluster match → falls to predictive_queue → discovery_question. Vendor asset layer adds: discovery_prompts

card.label: VENDOR DISCOVERY
card.asset: When you go from Chronograph's investment-level data to an investor distribution, how many manual steps are in between?
card.context: Maybern + Chronograph partnership. GTCR, One Rock, Serent all use Chronograph as complementary.

category_behavior.description: The vendor's category from the registry determines how the teleprompter treats the mention. Competitors get competitive responses. Systems of record get positioning. Integration partners get integration stories. Ancillary tools get discovery prompts.

competitor.tone: Differentiate without attacking. Frame as complementary or as solving a different problem.

system_of_record.tone: Position Maybern as the calculation layer that sits on top. Never suggest replacing the GL.
system_of_record.cluster_expected: Usually no cluster fires. Vendor asset layer activates independently.

integration_partner.tone: Reinforce the partnership. Show how the two systems work together.
integration_partner.cluster_expected: Usually no cluster fires. Vendor mention is a positive signal.

ancillary_tool.tone: Surface the validation burden the admin creates. Position Maybern as the verification layer.
ancillary_tool.cluster_expected: May fire pc-001 (Status Quo) if buyer defends admin relationship.

friendamie.tone: Complementary. Show how Maybern feeds data into their tool.

pipeline_position.description: Vendor asset integration runs AFTER Step 4 (Select Content) and BEFORE Step 5 (Apply Principle Filter). It enriches or replaces the content selected in Step 4 with a canonical asset if a vendor was detected.
pipeline_position.step_4_5_insertion: 4a. VENDOR ASSET ENRICHMENT: If a vendor was detected in the current segment, look up the vendor in canonical_conversation_assets. Use move_to_asset_mapping to select the appropriate asset category based on the recommended_move from Step 2. If a canonical asset exists, use it as the card content. If no canonical asset exists for this vendor, continue with the content selected in Step 4.
no_override_conditions: If the decision engine recommended_move is 'advancing_question' and the stage has a higher-priority unasked DQ from the scoring engine, the scored DQ takes precedence over the vendor discovery_prompt.
If the vendor is category 'friendamie', do not surface a card unless the rep is in a next-step or integration discussion.
If all canonical assets for this vendor + category have been shown in this session, fall back to the standard pipeline content from Step 4.

teleprompter_modes.description: Two user-selectable operating modes plus a question tracker and momentum suggestion card. These augment the existing teleprompter engine — they do not replace it. The underlying 10-step decision engine, pattern clusters, scoring logic, and all content remain unchanged. Modes control HOW content is presented, not WHAT content exists.

guided_meeting.label: Guided Meeting
guided_meeting.description: A structured meeting flow that guides the rep through the conversation in a deliberate order. Feels like a playbook. Best for newer reps or unfamiliar meeting types.

content_sequence.description: In Guided mode, the teleprompter walks the rep through content types in a fixed progression. Within each type, content is selected by the existing scoring engine and predictive queue priorities.

sequence.logic: Show the selected opening script. Rep delivers or edits. Mark complete when rep advances.
sequence.stage_gate: Only in open stages (m1-open, m2-open, m4-frame)

sequence.source: discovery_questions where question_type='advancing', ranked by priority_scoring engine
sequence.logic: Show the top-scored advancing question. When asked or skipped, advance to the next. Interleave reframes per predictive_queue ratio (every 2-3 questions).

sequence.source: reframing_lines array, matched by principle_tags to the most recently asked DQ
sequence.logic: Surface a reframe after the rep has asked 2-3 advancing questions. In Guided mode, the system prompts the transition explicitly: 'Consider reframing before the next question.'
After a reframe lands (buyer acknowledges risk), surface one proof point or credibility story. Deploy sparingly — max 2 per stage in Guided mode.

sequence.source: discovery_questions where question_type='confirming', ranked by priority_scoring engine
sequence.logic: When the question tracker shows 60%+ of advancing questions asked, the system prompts transition to confirming. Show the top-scored confirming question.
sequence.stage_gate: Only in confirming/advance stages, or when advancing questions are substantially complete

sequence.source: next_step_language array for the current stage
sequence.logic: After confirming questions verify the belief shift, surface the next-step language. In Guided mode, the system explicitly prompts: 'Ready to lock next steps.'

content_sequence.override_behavior: If a trigger or cluster fires at any point (Steps 0-7 of the decision engine), the triggered content takes priority over the Guided sequence. After the triggered card is handled, the Guided sequence resumes where it left off.

phase_tracking.description: The system tracks which phase the rep is in. Phase advances when the rep marks the current card as done (asked, delivered, or skipped). The rep can also manually jump to any phase.

live_copilot.label: Live Copilot
live_copilot.description: Reactive assistance without forcing a scripted sequence. Content appears only when signals, clusters, or predictive logic indicate relevance. The rep controls the flow. Feels like a lightweight live coach. Best for experienced reps.

content_behavior.description: In Copilot mode, the teleprompter does NOT walk through a fixed sequence. Instead, it surfaces content reactively based on three triggers, in priority order.

trigger_priority.source: Decision engine Steps 0-7 (trigger → cluster → decision_rules → content selection → vendor enrichment → scoring)
trigger_priority.behavior: When a trigger or cluster fires, surface the recommended card exactly as the decision engine produces it. This is identical behavior to Guided mode for triggered content.

When no explicit trigger fires but the buyer's recent speech contains a detectable signal or principle direction, surface the momentum card. This is the primary Copilot-mode intelligence — it reads conversational direction without requiring an explicit trigger match.
When neither a trigger nor a momentum signal is active, the predictive queue determines what to show. The first unused item in the queue for the current stage is surfaced.

content_types_surfaced: rebuttals — when buyer raises an objection or defends status quo
reframing_lines — when buyer describes a pain without recognizing it as a problem
credibility_stories — when buyer signals skepticism or needs social proof
proof_points — when buyer engages with a specific claim
integration_explanations — when a vendor is mentioned (via vendor_asset_integration)
positioning_responses — when a competitor is named
discovery_prompts — stage-aware fallback questions from the predictive queue
what_copilot_does_NOT_do: Does not force a question sequence
Does not prompt 'time to move to confirming questions'
Does not display an opening script unless the rep is in an open stage
Does not overwhelm with multiple cards — one card at a time, refreshed on each buyer turn

question_tracker.description: Displays which required questions have been asked and which remain for the current meeting and stage. Visible in both modes. Uses existing question item_ids and tags. Informational only — does not interrupt the meeting or block progression.

schema.asked_questions: array of item_ids marked as asked in this session
schema.skipped_questions: array of item_ids marked as skipped
schema.remaining_questions: array of item_ids not yet asked or skipped
schema.total_questions: integer — total DQs in the current stage
schema.category_progress: array of category objects showing topic coverage

category_definitions.description: Categories group questions by topic area using existing tags. Each DQ can appear in multiple categories. Categories are derived from methodology_tags, conversation_signals, and principle_tags — no new tagging required.

categories.label: Workflow & Process
categories.matching_rule: DQ has conversation_signals including ANY of: manual_process, workflow_fragility, shadow_models, excel_dependency

categories.label: Systems & Tools
categories.matching_rule: DQ has conversation_signals including ANY of: vendor_comparison, internal_build, excel_dependency

categories.label: Reporting & Visibility
categories.matching_rule: DQ has conversation_signals including ANY of: audit_risk, ownership_unclear

categories.label: Ownership & Key Person
categories.matching_rule: DQ has conversation_signals including ANY of: ownership_unclear, economic_buyer_missing

categories.label: Decision Process
categories.matching_rule: DQ has methodology_tags including ANY of: MEDDPICC-decision_process, MEDDPICC-paper_process

categories.label: Metrics & ROI
categories.matching_rule: DQ has methodology_tags including MEDDPICC-metrics OR conversation_signals including roi_curiosity

categories.label: Economic Buyer
categories.matching_rule: DQ has methodology_tags including MEDDPICC-economic_buyer OR conversation_signals including economic_buyer_missing

categories.label: Competition
categories.matching_rule: DQ has methodology_tags including MEDDPICC-competition OR conversation_signals including vendor_comparison

status_rules.complete: All DQs in the category for the current stage have been asked
status_rules.partial: At least one DQ in the category has been asked but others remain
status_rules.not_started: No DQs in the category have been asked yet

detection_method.description: Questions are marked as 'asked' when: (1) the rep manually marks them via the UI, OR (2) the speaker-filtered transcript contains phrasing substantially matching the DQ text. Detection is fuzzy — it matches semantic intent, not exact wording. A rep asking 'who owns the waterfall work?' matches dq-001 even though the exact text is different.
detection_method.manual_override: The rep can manually mark any question as asked or skipped at any time, regardless of transcript detection.

ui_behavior.location: Persistent sidebar panel in both modes. Always visible but collapsible.
ui_behavior.update_frequency: Re-computed whenever a question is marked asked or skipped.
ui_behavior.interaction: Tapping a category expands to show the specific DQs in that category and their status.

momentum_suggestion.description: A lightweight card that surfaces the best next move based on the buyer's most recent 1-2 turns. Reads conversational direction without requiring an explicit trigger or cluster match. Concise — one recommendation at a time. Defers to higher-priority triggered content when triggers fire.

schema.active_signal: string — the strongest conversation_signal detected in the last 1-2 buyer turns
schema.active_principle: string — the principle_tag most aligned to the detected signal (via signal_bridge and principle mappings)
schema.recommended_move: string — one of: advancing_question, confirming_question, reframe, proof_point, credibility_story, next_step_prompt
schema.recommended_item_id: string — the specific item_id to surface (DQ, reframe, proof point, etc.)
schema.display_text: string — the actual text shown on the card, taken from the recommended item's content
schema.confidence: string — HIGH, MEDIUM, or LOW based on signal strength
schema.source_utterance: string — the buyer statement that triggered this suggestion (for rep context)

detection_logic.description: The momentum engine runs after the speaker filter classifies a segment as 'client' and BEFORE checking whether a full trigger/cluster match exists. It is lighter-weight than the trigger layer — it looks for directional signals, not exact phrase matches.
steps: 1. EXTRACT SIGNAL: Scan the last 1-2 client segments for conversation_signal keywords. Use a soft match — the word 'quarter-end' suggests 'audit_risk' even without matching a full trigger phrase.

2. MAP TO PRINCIPLE: Use the DQ tag system to find which principle_tags are associated with the detected signal. If a DQ has conversation_signals=['audit_risk'] and principle_tags=['expose_hidden_risk'], then the momentum principle is 'expose_hidden_risk'.

3. SELECT MOVE: Based on the current stage and the momentum principle, select the recommended_move. Use the predictive_queue's phase progression as a guide — if the stage prioritizes discovery_question, recommend an advancing question. If confirming, recommend a confirming question.
4. SELECT ITEM: Use the priority_scoring engine to find the highest-scored unasked item that matches the momentum principle and recommended_move type.
5. COMPOSE CARD: Take the item's text, pair it with the detected signal and buyer utterance, and display as a single concise card.

confidence_levels.HIGH: 2+ signal keywords detected in the last buyer turn, AND the signal maps cleanly to an unasked DQ in the current stage
confidence_levels.MEDIUM: 1 signal keyword detected, OR the signal maps to a DQ in a related stage
confidence_levels.LOW: Weak signal — directional only. The momentum card shows but is dimmed.

priority_rules.defers_to: Any content from decision engine Steps 0-7 (triggered content). When a trigger or cluster fires, the momentum card is hidden and the triggered card takes its place.
priority_rules.defers_to_guided_phase: In Guided mode, the momentum card appears below the main Guided card as a secondary suggestion. It does not replace the Guided sequence.
priority_rules.primary_in_copilot: In Copilot mode, the momentum card is the PRIMARY intelligence source when no triggers fire. It fills the gap between triggered content and predictive queue fallback.

stage_discipline.rule: The momentum card must respect stage boundaries. It never surfaces content from a future stage. If the momentum signal maps to a question in m4-diagnose but the rep is in m1-advancing, the card does not show.
stage_discipline.cross_stage_exception: If the signal maps to a question that has meeting_relevance including the current meeting but stage_relevance in a different stage, the card shows with a dimmed indicator: 'This question is typically asked in [stage name].'

example_states.scenario: Buyer says 'Quarter-end gets messy' during M1/m1-advancing

momentum_suggestion.display_text: How long does your distribution process typically take from initial calculation to final wire?

example_states.scenario: Buyer says 'Our CFO would need to see this' during M4/m4-diagnose

momentum_suggestion.display_text: When your CFO evaluates an investment like this, what framework do they use — is it headcount comparison, time savings, risk reduction, or something else?
momentum_suggestion.source_utterance: Our CFO would need to see this.

example_states.scenario: Buyer says 'We're growing pretty fast' during M1/m1-advancing

momentum_suggestion.display_text: When you think about scaling from your current fund count to double that, which part of your operations would break first?

ui_behavior.card_position: Below the main content card in both modes. In Copilot mode, it IS the main card when no triggers fire.
ui_behavior.refresh: Re-computed on every new client speech segment that passes the speaker filter.
ui_behavior.persistence: The card stays visible until the next client segment arrives or the rep dismisses it.
ui_behavior.animation: Subtle fade-in (200ms). No jarring transitions during a live call.

mode_switching.description: The rep can switch between Guided and Copilot modes at any time during a session.
rules: Switching modes does NOT change the underlying meeting state (current_meeting, current_stage, dq_status, meddpicc_coverage).
Switching modes does NOT reset the question tracker. All asked/skipped states are preserved.
Switching modes does NOT alter clusters, predictive queues, scoring logic, or stage definitions.
Both modes rely on the same underlying teleprompter brain and 10-step decision engine.
The mode only changes HOW content is presented, not WHAT content exists.
Default mode for new sessions is configurable per rep (future: user_profile preference). Initial default: Guided.

guided_to_copilot: The fixed phase sequence disappears. No more 'Phase 2: Advancing Questions' header.
The momentum card becomes the primary intelligence source (instead of secondary).
Interleaved reframe prompts stop appearing automatically — reframes only surface when signals or momentum suggest them.
The question tracker remains visible and unchanged.
copilot_to_guided: The system determines which Guided phase the rep is in based on question tracker state: if <60% of advancing DQs are asked → Phase 2; if ≥60% → Phase 5 (confirming).
The fixed phase sequence resumes from the inferred phase.
The momentum card moves to secondary position (below the main Guided card).
The question tracker remains visible and unchanged.

mode_switching.ui_element: Toggle switch in the teleprompter header. Two states: 'Guided' (left) and 'Copilot' (right). Single tap to switch. No confirmation modal — speed matters.

engine_relationship.description: The modes, question tracker, and momentum card are a PRESENTATION LAYER that sits on top of the existing 10-step decision engine. They do not modify, replace, or bypass any step of the engine.

how_modes_use_the_engine.guided_mode: Uses the predictive_queue sequence as its primary content order (Step 8). Triggered content from Steps 0-7 overrides the Guided sequence when it fires. Scoring (Step 5-6) determines which specific item within a content type to show.
how_modes_use_the_engine.copilot_mode: Uses Steps 0-7 as the primary content source. When no trigger fires, the momentum card fills the gap. When neither trigger nor momentum fires, Step 8 (predictive_queue) provides the fallback.

session_script_system.description: Enables temporary in-session editing of opening statement scripts. Canonical scripts remain immutable. A session copy is created at meeting start, editable during the call, and discarded when the meeting ends.
session_script_system.scope: opening_statements in M1 (m1-open) and M2 (m2-open) only

canonical_scripts.description: The source-of-truth opening scripts. These are the existing opening_statements stored in the brain's meeting_types[].stages[]. They are NEVER modified by session edits.

registry.label: Appreciation + Structure Frame
registry.content_preview: Appreciate you making the time. Here's how I'd like to use the next 30 minutes...

registry.label: Curiosity Open — Why This Meeting
registry.content_preview: Before we get into anything, I'm curious — what made you take this meeting?

registry.label: About You, Not Us
registry.content_preview: I want to make this about your setup, not ours...

registry.label: Market Theme Frame
registry.content_preview: I've been meeting with firms like yours and there's a general theme I see...

registry.label: Context Before Product
registry.content_preview: Thanks for making the time. Before we get into the product — three minutes of context...

registry.label: Recap + Demo Bridge
registry.content_preview: Since our first conversation, we identified a few areas worth looking at more closely...

registry.label: Interactive Demo Frame
registry.content_preview: I want this to be interactive. If you see something relevant, say so...

session_script_schema.description: When a meeting session begins, the teleprompter creates a temporary editable copy of the selected canonical script. The rep can modify wording during the call. Edits persist only for the duration of the session.

schema.session_id: string — unique identifier for this call session (e.g., 'call_20260314_greystar_m1')
schema.created_at: ISO timestamp — when the session copy was created
schema.last_edited_at: ISO timestamp or null — when the rep last modified the text
schema.edit_count: integer — number of times the rep has modified this script in this session
schema.content: string — the full script text, initially a copy of the canonical script, modifiable by the rep

example.content: Appreciate you making the time, Patrick. I know you're in the middle of audit season so I'll keep this tight. Here's how I'd like to use the next 25 minutes: a few questions about how your team handles fund operations today, then a quick perspective on what we're seeing across real estate firms with similar complexity.

session_start.trigger: Rep starts a new meeting session in the teleprompter (selects meeting type M1 or M2).
session_start.action: Create a session_script by copying the content from the selected canonical script. The session_script is editable. The canonical script is untouched.
session_start.default_selection: The teleprompter presents all canonical scripts for the meeting type. The rep selects one. That selection creates the session copy.
session_start.if_no_selection: Default to the first canonical script for the meeting type (os-001 for M1, os-005 for M2).

during_session.editing: The rep can modify any part of the session_script.content field directly in the teleprompter UI. Edits are saved to the session_script in real time. Each edit increments edit_count and updates last_edited_at.
during_session.what_is_editable: Only the content text. The session_id, source_script_id, source_item_id, and temporary_override fields are read-only.
during_session.display: The teleprompter displays the session_script.content (not the canonical content) during the call. If the rep has not edited, the content is identical to the canonical version.

session_end.trigger: Rep ends the meeting session, closes the teleprompter, or starts a new meeting.
session_end.action: The session_script is deleted. It is not saved, archived, or persisted anywhere. The next session starts fresh from the canonical script.
session_end.data_retention: Zero. Session edits are ephemeral by design.

new_session.trigger: Rep starts a new meeting (even if with the same prospect).
new_session.action: A new session_script is created from the canonical script. No memory of prior session edits.

reset_behavior.description: At any point during a session, the rep can reset the session_script back to the canonical version.

reset_action.label: Reset to Default Opening
reset_action.behavior: Replace session_script.content with the canonical script's original content. Reset edit_count to 0. Reset last_edited_at to null.
reset_action.ui_element: A 'Reset to Default' button visible in the opening statement card. Requires single-click confirmation (not a modal — speed matters on a live call).

canonical_protection.rule: Session edits NEVER propagate to the canonical script. The canonical_scripts registry is immutable. Even if the rep resets, the canonical version is unchanged because it was never modified.
canonical_protection.enforcement: The canonical script is stored in the brain JSON (opening_statements). The session_script is stored in client-side session state only (JavaScript memory). There is no write path from session state back to the brain.

future_user_profile_scripts.description: Designed to be extended later with per-rep preferred opening scripts. NOT implemented in this version.

preferred_scripts.M1: script_id — which canonical script this rep prefers for M1
preferred_scripts.M2: script_id — which canonical script this rep prefers for M2

implementation_notes: When implemented, the session start logic would check for a user_profile_script before defaulting to the canonical script.
Priority order: user_profile_script > canonical_script > first script in array.
User profile scripts would persist across sessions (unlike session scripts).
User profile scripts would still NOT modify the canonical scripts.
future_user_profile_scripts.status: PLANNED — not implemented. Structure reserved for future use.

content_layer: 12 meetings (M1-M12) with 18 stages covering the full Maybern Sales Lifecycle
105 discovery questions with 4-layer tagging (question_type, methodology_tags, principle_tags, conversation_signals)
63 reframing lines, 45 proof points, 36 objection handling paths, 20 competitor response paths
44 top-level competitor responses, 54 top-level objection entries, 23 customer stories
83 trigger conditions with contextual multi-word matching (16 generic phrases expanded to 124 contextual alternatives)
95 predictive queue entries across all 18 stages aligned to Corporate Visions progression
14 pattern clusters with decision_rules (recommended_move, preferred_question_type, preferred_principle, escalation_move)
585 unique item_ids across all content types

pc-015 (EB Gatekeeping): New pattern cluster detecting champion shielding of economic buyer access. 55 buyer_language_examples across 4 signal categories (champion_shielding, champion_as_filter, proof_prerequisite, political_navigation). Linked to 11 existing DQs tagged MEDDPICC-economic_buyer and MEDDPICC-champion. Problem type pt-012 added. Signal bridge extended with 4 new entries. Priority weight 9 — outranks pc-011 (Executive Alignment, wt=7) when both match.

pc-016 (Buying Committee Risk): New pattern cluster detecting committee complexity, multi-stakeholder consensus requirements, hidden veto players, staged leadership exposure, internal blockers, stakeholder resistance, and deal velocity risk. 90+ buyer_language_examples. 10 committee_mapping_questions for political structure discovery. 5 committee_reframes with transition-to-question chaining. Signal bridge extended with 6 entries. Problem type pt-013 added. MEDDPICC focus: economic_buyer, decision_process, champion, decision_criteria. Stage relevance: m1-advancing, m1-confirming, m2-advancing, m3-core.
intelligence_layer: 10-step decision engine: speaker filter → cluster match → decision rules → stage filter → content selection → vendor enrichment → principle filter → type filter → escalation → predictive queue fallback
5-dimension priority scoring (stage match, signal cluster, principle, advancing/confirming fit, MEDDPICC relevance) — max 50 points
20-entry signal bridge translating DQ conversation_signals to cluster confidence_signals
Speaker detection with first-message classification, 1500ms turn alternation, 22-phrase consecutive-turn override, and 4-signal handoff detection
Vendor asset integration (Step 4a) with 6 move-to-asset mappings and 5 category-aware behavior rules
Trigger firing rules: contextual matching for generic terms, single-word for proper nouns, supporting_signal_only for ambiguous words

Response + Question Chaining: non-question moves (reframe, competitor_response, credibility_story, etc.) automatically attach a follow-up advancing question from the same cluster context. Pre-computed chain map for all 14 clusters. Stage-gated — no cross-stage chaining.

Cluster Memory: session-scoped conversation context tracking cluster activations, signal accumulation, topic progression, and vendor mentions. Enables repetition prevention, signal trend detection, depth tracking, context enrichment, and MEDDPICC gap awareness.

Cluster reinforcement: same-cluster repeat fires boost effective weight (+3 per repeat, max +6, 120s window). Content depth ladder shifts from DQs → proof points → confirming questions as reinforcement count rises. Resets on stage change.

Stage priority lock: hard block on future-stage content with stage_conversion_protocol (reframe + advancing question). Deferred signals auto-activate when the preferred stage is reached. Exceptions for competitor responses and objection handling.

Response + question chaining: non-question moves (reframes, competitor responses, credibility stories) automatically attach the top-scored advancing DQ from the same cluster as a 'THEN ASK' follow-up.

Story intelligence: metadata-driven story selection scoring across 5 dimensions (problem_pattern, customer_segment, system_stack, outcome_type, stage_relevance). Max 36 points. Session tracking prevents repetition.
Deal signal tracking: session-level signal accumulation with 6 defined signal-pair rules that dynamically boost MEDDPICC and principle scoring when compound patterns are detected.

Advancing vs confirming discipline: belief_shift_detected gate computed from question coverage (60% threshold), buyer acknowledgment phrases, and rep manual override. Confirming questions are invisible until the gate opens.
Predictive queue neutral-statement activation: PQ fires immediately on ambient buyer statements ('go ahead', 'tell me more') that match no triggers or signals.

Problem type registry: 10 diagnostic problem types (status_quo_defense, workflow_fragility, ownership_unclear, economic_buyer_distance, decision_process_unclear, roi_curiosity, competitor_evaluation, tool_stack_complexity, change_resistance, vision_readiness) inferred from accumulated signals and cluster activity. Each type specifies preferred_moves, avoid_moves, linked_item_ids, and scoring integration (+5 bonus for preferred, -5 penalty for avoided). Sits above signals/clusters as a diagnostic layer without modifying any existing system.

Workflow fragility signal expansion: 5 new signal types (quarter_end_pressure, month_end_pressure, reporting_cycle_stress, audit_preparation_stress, temporal_workload_spike) with 50+ detection phrases covering hyphenated variants and time-based reporting stress language. Signal bridge extended from 20 to 25 entries. pc-005 buyer_language_examples expanded from 20 to 50+ phrases. tr-009 trigger phrases expanded from 21 to 50+. All new signals map to existing cluster pc-005 and activate pt-002 (workflow_fragility).

Internal build signal expansion: 10 new signal types (internal_tool, homegrown_system, custom_solution, built_internally, internal_workflow_tool, internal_script, internal_dashboard, custom_reporting_tool, internal_process_tool, engineering_dependency) with 90+ detection phrases. Signal bridge extended to 35 entries. pc-004 buyer_language_examples expanded from 16 to 55+. tr-014 trigger phrases expanded from 4 to 40+. Dual-cluster mapping: pc-004 (Internal Build) for all signals, pc-005 (Workflow Fragility) for script/process/engineering dependency signals.

Admin dependency signal expansion: 14 new signal types (fund_admin, administrator_dependency, admin_managed_process, admin_runs_reports, admin_handles_reporting, admin_handles_that, admin_prepares_reports, admin_generates_reports, admin_maintains_books, admin_provides_reports, admin_runs_calculations, outsourced_reporting, outsourced_reconciliation, outsourced_financials) with 100+ detection phrases. Signal bridge extended to 49 entries. pc-008 buyer_language_examples expanded from 17 to 90+. tr-006 trigger phrases expanded from 13 to 70+.

Signal detection expansion based on 100-statement stress test: pc-001 (Status Quo) expanded from 13 to 90+ phrases covering hedged, polite, and indirect defensive language. pc-005 (Workflow) expanded with error narratives, time descriptions, fragility language, tool-stack integration gaps, and reporting stress phrases. pc-006 (Ownership) expanded with abstract ownership, delegation, key-person concentration, and decision authority language. pc-014 (Data Accessibility) expanded with multi-system fragmentation phrases. Signal bridge extended to 56 entries. tr-004 trigger phrases expanded to cover conversational status quo defense.
presentation_layer: Guided Meeting Mode: 6-phase structured playbook (open → advancing → reframe → prove → confirm → advance)
Live Copilot Mode: reactive assistance driven by momentum suggestion card when no triggers fire
Question Tracker: 10 categories from existing MEDDPICC and signal tags, persistent sidebar, complete/partial/not_started status
Momentum Suggestion Card: reads last 1-2 buyer turns, detects directional signals, surfaces one recommendation with HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW confidence
Session Script System: 7 canonical opening scripts (4 M1, 3 M2) with ephemeral session editing and reset-to-default
Mode switching preserves all session state

canonical_conversation_assets.json — 182 assets across 17 vendors, all under 300 characters
teleprompter_ui_logic.md — card selection, DQ tracking, trigger thresholds, tab layout

response_chaining.description: When the teleprompter surfaces a non-question move (reframe, competitor_response, vendor_response, credibility_story, integration_story), the system automatically attempts to attach a follow-up advancing question. This creates a response → question chain that keeps the conversation diagnostic after every non-question move. Does NOT modify any existing content — only links existing DQs dynamically.

chainable_moves.description: These move types always attempt to attach a follow-up advancing question.
non_chainable: advancing_question — already a question, no chain needed
confirming_question — already a question, no chain needed
roi_prompt — already a diagnostic prompt, no chain needed
next_step_lock — closing move, chaining a question would undercut the close

chain_selection_logic.description: How the system selects the follow-up advancing question for a chain. The question is selected from the current stage's unasked DQs using the active cluster's context.
steps: 1. CHECK CLUSTER LINKED DQs: If the active cluster has discovery_questions in its linked_item_ids, use the first unasked DQ from that list. These are hand-curated and highest quality.

2. SIGNAL MATCH: If no linked DQs exist (10 of 14 clusters have none), use the signal_bridge to find DQs whose conversation_signals overlap with the cluster's confidence_signals. Select the highest-scoring unasked advancing DQ via the priority_scoring engine.
3. PRINCIPLE MATCH: If no signal overlap produces a candidate, find the highest-scoring unasked advancing DQ whose principle_tags include the cluster's decision_rules.preferred_principle.
4. STAGE DEFAULT: If no principle match, use the first unasked advancing DQ in the current stage's array order.
5. NO CHAIN: If all advancing DQs in the current stage have been asked, do not attach a chain. The response stands alone.
chain_selection_logic.stage_gate: The chain question MUST belong to the current stage. Cross-stage chaining is never allowed.
chain_selection_logic.type_gate: The chain question MUST be question_type='advancing'. Confirming questions are never chained to non-question moves — they are reserved for stage boundaries.

chain_display.description: How the chained response + question pair appears on the teleprompter card.

layout.line_2: The primary response text (reframe, positioning response, etc.)
layout.line_5_dimmed: Question ID and principle tag (dimmed metadata for the rep)

example.scenario: Buyer says 'We manage most of this in Excel.' → pc-001 fires → reframe

card.label: REFRAME → THEN ASK
card.response: Every fund has an accounting book of record. The question is: what's your performance book of record? If the answer is Excel, that's a process, not a system.
card.chain_question: Walk me through who actually owns the waterfall logic today — and what happens to the next distribution cycle if that person is unavailable.

cluster_chain_map.description: Pre-computed best-fit chain DQs for each cluster. These are the first candidates checked in Step 1-2 of chain selection. If the candidate is already asked, the system falls through to Step 3-4.

pc-001.chain_logic: After reframing status quo, ask about ownership (dq-001), scaling (dq-006), or future projection (dq-012). All three surface hidden risk the buyer hasn't examined.

pc-002.chain_logic: After deflecting pricing, redirect to budget framing (dq-013), leadership concern (dq-007), or priority ranking (dq-011). Never justify the price — redirect to the cost of the status quo.

pc-003.chain_logic: After positioning against a competitor, ask about the admin relationship (dq-008), error history (dq-010), or evaluation criteria (dq-030). Shift the frame from vendor comparison to decision criteria.

pc-004.chain_logic: After validating the internal build, ask about key person risk (dq-005), structural limitations (dq-004), or ownership (dq-001). Surface the rigidity cost without attacking.

pc-005.chain_logic: Cluster already recommends advancing_question — chaining applies only if the move was escalated to a reframe. After reframe, return to the discovery thread.

pc-006.chain_logic: After reframing key person risk, ask directly about the departure scenario. High-emotion questions — let the weight land.

pc-007.chain_logic: After engaging with scaling, ask the future projection question. Bypass the 'we're fine' defense by projecting into a future the buyer hasn't stress-tested.

pc-008.chain_logic: After reframing admin dependency, ask about the admin relationship quality (dq-008), error catching (dq-010), or data access latency (dq-014).

pc-009.chain_logic: Stall cluster recommends confirming_question — no advancing chain. Confirming questions are not chained.

pc-010.chain_logic: If ROI prompt escalates to reframe in M1, chain to budget framing (dq-013) or leadership concern (dq-007).

pc-011.chain_logic: After engaging with executive alignment, ask about leadership framework (dq-007) or budget positioning (dq-013).

pc-012.chain_logic: After addressing implementation fear, ask about defensibility (dq-002) or distribution timeline (dq-009). Ground the conversation in operational specifics.

pc-013.chain_logic: After mapping decision process, ask about leadership involvement (dq-007) or budget approach (dq-013).

pc-014.chain_logic: After reframing data accessibility, ask about data request latency (dq-014) or distribution timeline (dq-009).

cluster_memory.description: Lightweight conversation context that tracks cluster activations, buyer statements, signal accumulation, and topic progression across the session. Enables the teleprompter to avoid repeating content, build on prior context, and detect when the buyer's concerns are shifting. Does NOT modify any existing architecture — this is a runtime data structure that the presentation layer reads.

cluster_history.description: Ordered list of every cluster activation during the session. Includes what the buyer said, when it fired, and what the teleprompter surfaced in response.

entry_schema.buyer_utterance: string — the buyer statement that triggered this cluster
entry_schema.detected_signals: array of conversation_signals detected in the utterance
entry_schema.recommended_move: string — what the decision engine recommended
entry_schema.surfaced_item_id: string — what was actually shown to the rep
entry_schema.chain_item_id: string or null — the chained DQ if response chaining fired
entry_schema.rep_used: boolean — whether the rep marked the card as used (asked/delivered)

signal_accumulation.description: Running count of each conversation_signal detected across all buyer turns. Signals accumulate — they are never decremented. This reveals the dominant themes of the conversation.

cluster_activation_counts.description: How many times each cluster has been activated in the session. Used to detect repetition and prevent the same cluster from dominating the conversation.

topic_progression.description: Tracks the high-level topic evolution: what the conversation started with, what it's currently focused on, and where it's trending. Derived from signal_accumulation trends.

fields.opening_topic: string — the dominant signal from the first 3 buyer turns
fields.current_topic: string — the dominant signal from the last 2 buyer turns
fields.topic_shifts: array of {from, to, at_turn} — records when the buyer's focus changed

vendor_mentions.description: Array of vendors detected during the session, with timestamps and the canonical assets that were shown.

entry_schema.assets_shown: array of {asset_category, asset_index} tracking which canonical assets have been surfaced

repetition_prevention.description: Prevents the teleprompter from repeating the same type of response when a cluster fires multiple times.
repetition_prevention.rule: If a cluster has activated 2+ times and the same recommended_move was surfaced both times, escalate to the cluster's escalation_move on the third activation.
repetition_prevention.example: pc-001 fires twice → reframe shown both times. Third time → escalate to advancing_question instead of showing a third reframe.

signal_trend_detection.description: Detects when the buyer's concerns are shifting and adjusts the momentum suggestion accordingly.
signal_trend_detection.rule: If the dominant signal in the last 2 buyer turns differs from the dominant signal in the first 3 turns, the momentum card should prioritize the NEW signal direction. The buyer is moving — follow them.
signal_trend_detection.example: Conversation started with status_quo_confidence (buyer defending process). Last two turns shifted to ownership_unclear (buyer revealing key person risk). Momentum card should surface a key-person question, not another status quo reframe.

depth_tracking.description: Tracks how deeply a topic has been explored. If the buyer has given 3+ answers about workflow_fragility, the system should shift to a new topic dimension rather than continuing to probe the same area.
depth_tracking.rule: When signal_accumulation for a single signal exceeds 3, reduce the priority of DQs associated with that signal by 3 points in the scoring engine. This soft-deprioritizes explored topics without hard-blocking them.
depth_tracking.implementation: This is a scoring modifier, not a scoring engine change. The 5 existing dimensions remain unchanged. The depth penalty is applied as a post-scoring adjustment.

context_enrichment.description: When a cluster fires, the memory provides context from prior activations to enrich the teleprompter card.
context_enrichment.rule: If the same cluster fired earlier in the session and the rep delivered a response, the card for the second activation should acknowledge the prior context: 'Earlier you mentioned [X]. Building on that...'
context_enrichment.implementation: The card's context line (line_4 in card_composition) pulls from cluster_history to reference the buyer's earlier statement. This is a display enhancement, not a content change.

meddpicc_gap_awareness.description: Cross-references cluster_memory with the question_tracker's MEDDPICC coverage to identify which MEDDPICC elements have been ignored despite the conversation providing openings.
meddpicc_gap_awareness.rule: If signal_accumulation shows economic_buyer_missing at 2+ but the question_tracker shows 0 economic_buyer DQs asked, the momentum card should flag this gap: 'EB hasn't been identified yet. Consider asking: [dq-007]'
meddpicc_gap_awareness.implementation: The momentum card reads from both cluster_memory.signal_accumulation and teleprompter_modes.question_tracker.category_progress. When a signal has accumulated 2+ but its MEDDPICC category is 'not_started', the gap is flagged.

lifecycle.initialization: Created when a meeting session starts. All counts at zero. No history.
lifecycle.update_frequency: Updated on every client speech segment that passes the speaker filter. Cluster history appended on every cluster activation. Signal accumulation incremented on every signal detection.
lifecycle.persistence: Session-only. Deleted when the meeting ends. Not persisted across sessions.

cluster_history.buyer_utterance: We do most of the reconciliation manually in spreadsheets.
Our admin handles all the waterfall calculations.

cluster_reinforcement.description: When the same pattern cluster fires multiple times within a short window, the system increases its effective priority weight. This causes the decision engine to surface deeper content for that pattern — later-sequence DQs, stronger reframes, and more specific proof points. Memory resets when the meeting stage changes.
